posture: Quiet Saturday scan with the §1e 50-council deep dive running. The free §1a-§1d sources produced no fresh, in-window, in-scope innovation signal; the sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but recycled older or undated coverage. The watchlist deep scan across newsrooms and the latest available business papers / Notices of Motion also surfaced nothing publishable in-window.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a-§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. As this is a Saturday, the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan was additionally run across newsrooms and the latest available council/committee business papers and Councillor Notices of Motion; yield was lumpy as expected and produced no in-window loggable signal. Several recurring or undated items were again considered and excluded: the NSW commercial FOGO mandate (business-tier obligations commenced 1 July) is a regulatory-rollout milestone, not a fresh innovation signal; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial and its associated FOGO deferral resurfaced only via older (November 2025) coverage; the City of Sydney / Goterra residential insect-farming food-waste trial dates to late 2024-January 2025 and is well out-of-window; the City of Casey 'Smart Casey Program 2026-2029' roadmap was reported in late May and is out-of-window; the NSW AI-in-planning tools (Early Adopter Grant Program, DAISY, AI Solutions Panel) produced no new dated development; and overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 retrospectives, US battery/waste-to-energy market notes) was either out-of-window, a market/policy note rather than a transferable council signal, or general awards/retrospective coverage. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used.
